title: Onboarding flow architecture (step machine, resumable state)
description: Design and implement the onboarding step state machine. Stores current step in @AppStorage so it resumes after app restart. Steps: login → mic → screen-recording → video-platform → test-recording → ready. Allows skipping non-essential steps. Pablo Bear guides each step.

title: Onboarding Step 2: Microphone permission request + verification
description: Build OnboardingMicView. Request NSMicrophoneUsageDescription permission. Show current status and guide user to System Settings if denied. Pablo Bear copy: 'Pablo needs mic access to listen in sessions'. Verify permission is granted before advancing.

title: Onboarding Step 1: Account connection / login screen
description: Build OnboardingLoginView. Options: enter Pablo API credentials or OAuth via browser. On success, store auth token in Keychain (following audiotake2 auth pattern). Show Pablo Bear with warm intro copy. Advance to mic step on success.

title: Add AudioCaptureKit as Swift package dependency
description: Based on audit (PABLO-D-010), add AudioCaptureKit to the Xcode project — either as an SPM package pointing to pablo-health/audiotake2 or as a git submodule. Configure any required entitlements (microphone, screen recording) in Info.plist.
close_reason: Stale bead — feature shipped and verified in code (companion audit 2026-06-01). Foundation, AudioCaptureKit/RecordingService, core session UI (day-view, row, start-session, empty state), backend sync (APIClient, fetch-today, upload-on-end, create endpoints), video launch (Zoom/Teams/Meet), login screen, and Windows foundation/session-UI/WASAPI audio are all implemented on the relevant platform(s). Git log corroborates (#63-#72).

title: Implement RecordingService wrapper around AudioCaptureKit
description: Create mac/Sources/Services/RecordingService.swift — a @MainActor class wrapping AudioCaptureKit. Exposes async startRecording(sessionId:) and stopRecording() -> RecordingResult. Handles permission checks and publishes recording state.
close_reason: Stale bead — feature shipped and verified in code (companion audit 2026-06-01). Foundation, AudioCaptureKit/RecordingService, core session UI (day-view, row, start-session, empty state), backend sync (APIClient, fetch-today, upload-on-end, create endpoints), video launch (Zoom/Teams/Meet), login screen, and Windows foundation/session-UI/WASAPI audio are all implemented on the relevant platform(s). title: Audit audiotake2 repo — identify public API surface
description: Explore pablo-health/audiotake2 to document: (1) AudioCaptureKit public Swift API, (2) existing Pablo branding assets so we don't duplicate them, (3) macOS deployment target, (4) how auth is handled, (5) whether it's suitable as an SPM package or needs submodule. Output a short audit doc.
close_reason: Audit complete. AudioCaptureKit is a remote SPM package (github.com/lll-solutions/AudioCaptureKit). Auth uses ASWebAuthenticationSession + Keychain + Firebase token refresh. No Pablo branding in audiotake2 — generic sample. macOS 14.0, Swift 6.2. MVVM structure already in place.

title: Initialize Xcode project (SwiftUI, macOS target, SPM)
description: Create the Xcode project under mac/ with SwiftUI app template, macOS 14+ deployment target, Swift 6.0, and Swift Package Manager configured. No storyboards.
close_reason: Xcode project copied from audiotake2/sample/macos-sample and renamed to PabloCompanion. Builds and runs successfully. Bundle ID: health.pablo.companion. Swift 6.2, macOS 14.0 target.

title: Set up MVVM folder structure
description: Create Models/ViewModels/Views/Services folder structure under mac/Sources/. Add .gitkeep files so the structure is committed. Matches architecture defined in CLAUDE.md.

title: Practice harness: stream trailing silence so end-of-turn fires
description: FileAudioInputSource replays the fixture then stops emitting frames the instant the audio ends. Real microphone capture keeps delivering (near-silent) frames after the speaker stops, and that trailing silence is what the streaming ASR uses to detect end-of-turn and finalize a transcript. Our speech fixtures end mid-utterance with no trailing silence, so the turn never closes: ASR recognizes nothing, the patient never responds, and no note is produced. Fix: have FileAudioInputSource emit a configurable tail of silent 20ms frames after the fixture (default ~1.5s) before reporting didFinish, mirroring an open mic. Found by running the headless harness against the deployed backend.
notes: Fix implemented + validated headless against dev (ASR finalizes, patient audio returns). PR #74 open, blocked on required review approval (branch policy; cannot self-approve).

title: Both: register install_id with backend during OAuth code exchange
description: During the existing OAuth code-exchange step (LoginView / KeychainManager flow on Mac, OAuth handler on Windows), generate a per-install UUID install_id (stored in Keychain on Mac, Credential Manager on Windows) and POST {install_id, platform, os_version, hostname_hash} to the backend alongside the auth code. Backend (THERAPY-xo0o) persists it and binds the refresh token to install_id. install_id is generated once at first OAuth and persists across sign-outs (it identifies the install, not the session). Hostname_hash is SHA-256 of hostname so we can spot duplicates without storing PII. No-op for the user — invisible part of the existing flow. Lays groundwork for per-device revocation and (later) DPoP signing.

title: Both: launch-intent redemption + 'Start session with [Patient Name]?' confirmation UI
description: Wire the new https://app.pablo.health/launch/<intent_id> handoff into the existing DeepLinkRouter on both platforms. On receipt: (1) POST /launch/redeem {intent_id} with current access token; (2) on success backend returns {appointment_id, patient_name, video_url, session_id}; (3) open an ephemeral session window showing 'Start session with [Patient Name]?' as a prominent affirmative confirmation; (4) only on the therapist's explicit Start Recording tap does the mic arm. Existing pablohealth://session/start handler stays as Firefox/fallback path and follows the same confirmation UI flow. Mac: extend DeepLinkRouter.swift + new SessionConfirmationView.swift. Windows: extend DeepLinkRouter.cs + new SessionConfirmationView.xaml. CRITICAL: never auto-arm the microphone from an external trigger — affirmative tap inside the trusted app is the consent gate. Design doc §'Deep-link handoff'.

title: Windows: add uap3:AppUriHandler to Package.appxmanifest for app.pablo.health
description: Extend windows/PabloCompanion/Package.appxmanifest with the uap3 namespace and an AppUriHandler extension claiming https://app.pablo.health. The backend serves /.well-known/web-credentials listing this package's PFN (tracked as THERAPY-5w65 in pablo-saas), so the OS verifies us as the rightful handler on install/update. When wired, Edge and Chrome route https://app.pablo.health/launch/* directly to the companion via the existing ProtocolActivationListener — no scheme prompt, no spoofing. Firefox falls back to the existing pablohealth:// scheme. The current pablohealth:// protocol registration stays for OAuth callback + Firefox fallback. Pseudocode for manifest addition is in design doc §'Platform requirements'.

title: Mac: add Associated Domains entitlement for applinks:app.pablo.health (Universal Links)
description: Add com.apple.developer.associated-domains entitlement to mac/PabloCompanion/PabloCompanion.entitlements with value applinks:app.pablo.health. When the backend serves the AASA file at https://app.pablo.health/.well-known/apple-app-site-association (tracked as THERAPY-5w65 in pablo-saas), this lets macOS route https://app.pablo.health/launch/* URLs directly to PabloCompanion via the existing onOpenURL handler in PabloCompanionApp.swift. Replaces the current pablohealth:// scheme as the primary handoff path; the custom scheme remains for OAuth callback and as a Firefox fallback. Test: install signed build, click https://app.pablo.health/launch/test in Safari, confirm companion launches and DeepLinkRouter receives the URL. Design doc: pablo-health/pablo/docs/design/companion-thin-client.md §'Platform requirements'.

title: EPIC: Thin-client companion + secure deep-link handoff
description: Companion becomes a thin handoff target with a minimal main window. Existing native dashboard / day view / patient list / session history code is preserved behind ENABLE_NATIVE_DASHBOARD feature flag (default false) for future revival. New flow: user lives on web dashboard at pablo.health; Start Session button hands off to companion via domain-verified deep link (Universal Link on Mac, App URI Handler on Windows MSIX); companion shows 'Start session with [Patient Name]?' confirmation before arming mic. Companion-side tracking epic for the change. Backend + frontend work tracked in pablo-saas under THERAPY-jnbf. Full design doc: pablo-health/pablo/docs/design/companion-thin-client.md.

title: Onboarding Step 5: Test recording (5-second capture + playback)
description: Build OnboardingTestRecordingView. Press-to-record 5 seconds, then play back immediately so therapist confirms audio is working. Uses RecordingService. Shows waveform animation during capture. Pablo Bear celebrates success.

title: Onboarding Step 6: 'You're ready' — Pablo Bear celebration + day view hand-off
description: Build OnboardingReadyView. Pablo Bear celebration moment — warm copy, maybe a subtle animation. 'You're all set\! Let's see today's sessions.' Button lands on the day view. Mark onboarding complete in @AppStorage.

title: Onboarding Step 4: Video platform detection + configuration
description: Build OnboardingVideoView. Detect installed apps (Zoom, Teams, Meet). Let therapist confirm their preferred platform. Store in @AppStorage. Pablo Bear copy: 'Which video platform do you use for sessions?'. Skippable — can configure later in Settings.

title: Onboarding Step 3: Screen recording / system audio permission
description: Build OnboardingScreenRecordingView. Required if AudioCaptureKit captures system audio. Check TCC permission status. Guide user to System Settings > Privacy > Screen Recording. Make skippable if only mic capture is needed (confirm in audiotake2 audit).

title: Epic 5: Video Conferencing Launch

PHISanitizer.stripsSSN drops SSN redaction — DATE regex clobbers SSN match
description: PHISanitizerTests.stripsSSN() fails: input 'SSN: 123-45-6789' (or similar) sanitizes to 'SSN: 1[DATE]' instead of 'SSN: [SSN]'. The DATE pattern is matching part of the SSN before/instead of the SSN pattern, so SSN digits leak through redaction. This is a PHI/HIPAA-relevant correctness bug in production sanitization, pre-existing on main (not caused by PABLO-D-zzx). Fix: reorder/ tighten regex precedence so SSN is matched before DATE, add regression test. See PHISanitizerTests.swift:39.

title: Both: minimal main window (status + Open Web Dashboard button) behind ENABLE_NATIVE_DASHBOARD flag
description: Add a runtime feature flag ENABLE_NATIVE_DASHBOARD (default false). When false (the new shape): authenticated ContentView renders a minimal ~480x360pt window with — (a) status block: 'Connected to pablo.health as <email>' + green/red dot, mic-ready indicator, update-available indicator; (b) primary CTA: 'Open Web Dashboard' button (NSWorkspace.shared.open on Mac, Launcher.LaunchUriAsync on Windows) → https://app.pablo.health/dashboard; (c) footer with account/sign-out/preferences/version. When true: today's four-tab shell (today/sessions/patients/settings) is restored verbatim. CRITICAL: no view files deleted. DayView.swift, SessionHistoryView.swift, PatientListView.swift, their view models and tests all stay — the flag just chooses between two top-level shells. Recording surface (RecordingControlsView / PracticeSessionView) still opens as an ephemeral window during sessions, independent of the flag. Settings still accessible from the minimal window footer. Design doc §'Companion UI changes'.

title: PHISanitizer.stripsSSN flaky under parallel test execution
description: PHISanitizerTests 'stripsSSN' fails intermittently in the full Swift Testing run (result 'SSN: 1[DATE]' — the [DATE] regex eats the SSN digits) but PASSES when the PHISanitizer suite runs in isolation (xcodebuild -only-testing:PabloTests/PHISanitizer). Strongly suggests shared mutable state / thread-safety bug in PHISanitizer's pattern application under Swift Testing's parallel execution. Pre-existing on main (merge-base 2679cad); main CI currently green so it's intermittent. HIPAA-relevant (SSN may be left unredacted). Out of scope for the companion E2E harness work where it was found. Repro: run full PabloTests suite a few times.

title: Remove dead single-file recording-upload code (/api/recordings/upload)
description:
Both clients carry an unused single-file upload method targeting POST /api/recordings/upload that nothing invokes and that has no server route:

  - mac: APIClient.uploadRecording (Services/APIClient.swift) is wrapped by UploadViewModel.uploadRecording, but no View ever calls it. ContentView keeps UploadViewModel only for backend-URL config, auth wiring, and the health check. UploadViewModel's own doc string still says it uploads 'to the sample backend' — leftover prototype scaffolding.
  - windows: APIClient.UploadRecordingAsync has zero callers.

The live recording path on both platforms is the dual-channel POST /api/sessions/{id}/upload-audio (mac TranscriptionViewModel.uploadAudio, windows UploadAudioAsync), which is the only audio upload the running app performs.

Delete the dead code so the recordings/upload path doesn't read as a real endpoint:
  - mac: APIClient.uploadRecording + UploadViewModel.uploadRecording (and any now-unused response model / progress plumbing only it used). Keep UploadViewModel's backend-URL / auth / health responsibilities, or fold them elsewhere if uploadRecording was its only upload reason.
  - windows: APIClient.UploadRecordingAsync (+ its UploadResponse usage if unused elsewhere).

Pure cleanup, no behaviour change to the live upload flow. Verify the mac + windows builds and existing TranscriptionViewModel tests still pass.
close_reason: Done. Merged via PR #72 (squash 2679cad).
